steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question. The blank must describe what recording artists do to distinctions between Indigenous music and other styles. The examples — Tagaq blending Inuit throat singing into electronic music and Pura Fé combining Indigenous rhythms with R&B — show artists actively crossing genre boundaries, not honoring or maintaining them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — reject: to refuse to accept or honor something — the artists refuse to honor genre boundaries by freely blending styles together. ✓
  • Choice B — disguise: to conceal the true nature of something — the artists are not hiding that they mix styles; they openly name their cross-genre albums. ✗
  • Choice C — replace: to substitute one thing for another — the artists are not substituting other styles for Indigenous music; they are combining both at once. ✗
  • Choice D — observe: to notice or follow (a rule or boundary) — if the artists observed distinctions they would keep styles separate, the opposite of what the examples show. ✗
A is correct.
